title: Methods for Collecting and Analyzing Post-Ejaculatory Uterine Fluid in Mice
author: Yu Matsumoto

steps.title: Breed ICR strain male and female...
steps.instruction: Breed ICR strain male and female mice separately (1–5 mice per cage) under the following specific pathogen-free conditions: controlled temperature (23 ± 1 °C), humidity (40%–60%), light/dark cycles (lights on at 5 am and off at 7 pm), and ad libitum access to food and water ( Figure 1A ). Figure

steps.title: Mating male and female mice
steps.instruction: Mating male and female mice. (A) Experimental flow. (B) Male and female mice housed together. (C) Refusal of mounting behavior. (D) Allowance of mounting behavior. (E–H) Copulatory plugs checked. (F) Vagina of a female (during proestrus, metestrus, or diestrus) that refused mounting behavior. (G) Vagina of a female (during estrus) that allowed mounting behavior. The arrow indicates swelling of the vulva. (H) Vagina of a female post-ejaculation. B. Mating, determining estrous mice, and assessing copulatory plugs

steps.title: House male and female mice (8...
steps.instruction: House male and female mice (8 weeks old) together between 9 and 10 am ( Figure 1B ). Note: Ad libitum access to food and water is suspended during this time.

steps.title: Check mounting behavior on the female...
steps.instruction: Check mounting behavior on the female ( Figure 1C and D). Note: If there is no mounting behavior about 10 min after housing mice, change male or female. Outside estrous females refuse mounting behavior ( Video 1 ), whereas estrous females allow it ( Video 2 ). Video

steps.title: Mating male and female outside estrus Video
steps.instruction: Mating male and female outside estrus Video

steps.title: Mating male and female in estrus
steps.instruction: Mating male and female in estrus

steps.title: Check ejaculation into the female. Note
steps.instruction: Check ejaculation into the female. Note: Ejaculation can be confirmed by the male’s temporary rigidity while holding the female after mounting behavior with anteroposterior movement of the loin ( Video 1 ).

steps.title: Lift the base of the female’s...
steps.instruction: Lift the base of the female’s tail and check for a copulatory plug attached to the vagina using tweezers ( Figure 1E –H). Note: If no mounting behavior is observed about 10 min after housing mice, change male or female. In females post-ejaculation, a copulatory plug can be observed blocking the vaginal opening. This plug formation occurs within minutes after ejaculation is confirmed. C. Collection of eUF

steps.title: Place scissors and tweezers on the...
steps.instruction: Place scissors and tweezers on the bench and wear gloves ( Figure 2A ).

steps.title: Sacrifice females post-ejaculation. Note
steps.instruction: Sacrifice females post-ejaculation. Note: As a method of euthanasia, hold the base of the tail with one hand, place the thumb and index finger of the other hand on the neck, and quickly pull both sides (head and tail side) to perform cervical dislocation. For the exact time of ejaculation, refer to step B

steps.title: Disinfect the abdomen with 70% ethanol...
steps.instruction: Disinfect the abdomen with 70% ethanol and cut the skin using large scissors ( Figure 2B ).

steps.title: Pull apart the two sides (head...
steps.instruction: Pull apart the two sides (head and tail side) of the cut skin to access the peritoneum ( Figure 2C ).

steps.title: Cut the peritoneum using small scissors...
steps.instruction: Cut the peritoneum using small scissors ( Figure 2D ) and find the female reproductive tract (V-shaped uterus, oviducts, and ovaries) using tweezers ( Figure 2E ).

steps.title: Remove the bladder and adipose tissue...
steps.instruction: Remove the bladder and adipose tissue around the bladder using small scissors and tweezers ( Figure 2F ).

steps.title: Cut the endocervix using small scissors...
steps.instruction: Cut the endocervix using small scissors ( Figure 2G ) and hold it using tweezers ( Figure 2H ).

steps.title: Remove adipose tissue and blood from...
steps.instruction: Remove adipose tissue and blood from the uterus using tweezers and Kimwipes and excise a portion of the uterus ( Figure 2I ).

steps.title: Collect eUF in a 1
steps.instruction: Collect eUF in a 1.5 mL tube using a P-200 pipette ( Figures 2J –L). Note: By following steps C1–C9, pre-ejaculatory uterine fluid (UF) can be collected from estrus females (steps B1 and B2). Section C (collection of eUF) can be performed in approximately 5 min. Figure

steps.title: Collection of post-ejaculatory uterine fluid (eUF)
steps.instruction: Collection of post-ejaculatory uterine fluid (eUF). (A) Tools for abdominal exploration. (B) Sacrificed female mice post-ejaculation. (C) Peritoneum accessed. (D) Cut peritoneum. (E) The location of the female reproductive tract. (F) Removal of the bladder and adipose tissue. (G, H) Cut endocervix. (I) A small part of the uterus is cut open. (J) A P-200 pipette with a 200 μL tip is inserted into the uterus. (K) Collection of eUF from the uterus. (L) UF and eUF collected from the uterus. D. Preparation of protein concentration for SDS-PAGE

steps.title: Prepare UF and eUF diluted 10×...
steps.instruction: Prepare UF and eUF diluted 10× and 30× with distilled water. Note: When the viscosity of eUF is high, it is extremely difficult to measure the appropriate amount with a pipette. In such cases, add distilled water to the eUF to decrease its viscosity before preparing the sample.

steps.title: Prepare a dilution series of 1, 0
steps.instruction: Prepare a dilution series of 1, 0.5, 0.25, 0.125, 0.0625, and 0.03125 mg/mL albumin using bovine serum albumin standard ampules (2 mg/mL).

steps.title: Prepare samples and a dilution series...
steps.instruction: Prepare samples and a dilution series in a 96-well plate using the Protein Assay BCA kit.

steps.title: Measure the absorbance of samples and...
steps.instruction: Measure the absorbance of samples and a dilution series using the SpectraMax iD

steps.title: Prepare a calibration curve with absorbance...
steps.instruction: Prepare a calibration curve with absorbance on the x-axis and protein concentration on the y-axis, and a calibration curve of the dilution series using Excel.

steps.title: Identify the protein concentration of UF...
steps.instruction: Identify the protein concentration of UF and eUF using the calibration curve as an indicator.

steps.title: Adjust the protein concentrations of UF...
steps.instruction: Adjust the protein concentrations of UF and eUF (1 μg/μL and 1× sample buffer) using 6× sample buffer and distilled water.

steps.title: Store samples at -80 °C until use
steps.instruction: Store samples at -80 °C until use. E. Preparation of acrylamide gel (8%) for SDS-PAGE

steps.title: Set the seal gasket on the...
steps.instruction: Set the seal gasket on the glass plates and assemble the gel caster using clips.

steps.title: Insert the comb into the gel...
steps.instruction: Insert the comb into the gel caster and place a mark 5 mm below the comb.

steps.title: Wear gloves and prepare stacking and...
steps.instruction: Wear gloves and prepare stacking and separation gel solutions in 15 mL tubes.

steps.title: Add 25% (w/v) APS (25 μL)...
steps.instruction: Add 25% (w/v) APS (25 μL) to the separation gel solution.

steps.title: Apply the separation gel solution containing...
steps.instruction: Apply the separation gel solution containing APS up to the mark into the gel caster. Note: After adding 25% APS to the separation gel solution, mix well and promptly add the solution to the gel caster.

steps.title: Slowly add 1 mL of distilled...
steps.instruction: Slowly add 1 mL of distilled water using a P-1000 pipette into the gel caster. 7.When the gel hardens, tilt the gel caster and remove the distilled water using Kimwipes.

steps.title: Add 25% (w/v) APS (12
steps.instruction: Add 25% (w/v) APS (12.5 μL) to stacking gel solution.

steps.title: Apply stacking gel solution containing APS...
steps.instruction: Apply stacking gel solution containing APS into the gel caster and insert a comb. Note: After adding APS to the stacking gel solution, mix well and promptly add the solution to the gel caster.

steps.title: When the gel hardens, cover it...
steps.instruction: When the gel hardens, cover it with plastic wrap and store at 4 °C until use. F. Comparison of the protein composition between UF and eUF using SDS-PAGE

steps.title: Add 8 M urea in distilled...
steps.instruction: Add 8 M urea in distilled water (5 μL) to samples (12.5 μL; step D

steps.title: and incubate at 95 °C for 5 min
steps.instruction: and incubate at 95 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: After incubation, add 6× sample buffer (1 μL)
steps.instruction: After incubation, add 6× sample buffer (1 μL).

steps.title: Remove the comb and clips from...
steps.instruction: Remove the comb and clips from the gel caster and place the gel in the electrophoresis system.

steps.title: Fill the electrophoresis system with running buffer
steps.instruction: Fill the electrophoresis system with running buffer.

steps.title: Wash the inside of the wells...
steps.instruction: Wash the inside of the wells using a P-200 pipette with a 200 μL tip.

steps.title: Apply the samples (18
steps.instruction: Apply the samples (18.5 μL) and protein molecular weight marker (18.5 μL; protein molecular weight marker: 10 μL; 1× sample buffer: 2.5 μL; 6× sample buffer: 1 μL; 8 M urea in distilled water: 5 μL) in the wells using a P-20 pipette with a gel loading tip.

steps.title: Apply 1× sample buffer containing urea...
steps.instruction: Apply 1× sample buffer containing urea in the empty wells (18.5 μL; 1× sample buffer: 12.5 μL; 6× sample buffer: 1 μL; 8M urea in distilled water: 5 μL).

steps.title: Allow the gel to electrophorese (select mode
steps.instruction: Allow the gel to electrophorese (select mode: Tris-Gly/PAGEL, 1 gel, 80 min).

steps.title: After electrophoresis, place the gel in...
steps.instruction: After electrophoresis, place the gel in a case containing CBB staining solution and stain for 1 h at room temperature (25–27 °C) on a shaking apparatus.

steps.title: Wash the gel with tap water...
steps.instruction: Wash the gel with tap water and decolorize with CBB decolorizing solution. Note: eUF proteins separate more efficiently upon the addition of urea ( Figure 3 ). Figure

steps.title: SDS-PAGE of post-ejaculatory uterine fluid (eUF)
steps.instruction: SDS-PAGE of post-ejaculatory uterine fluid (eUF). Results of SDS-PAGE of UF and eUF samples with urea (right image) or without urea (left image). Arrow: clumping proteins. G. Histological analysis of the uterus post-ejaculation using hematoxylin-eosin (H&E) staining

steps.title: Prepare females post-ejaculation (steps B1–
steps.instruction: Prepare females post-ejaculation (steps B1–

steps.title: as well as the dissection environment (step C1)
steps.instruction: as well as the dissection environment (step C1).

steps.title: Sacrifice females (steps C
steps.instruction: Sacrifice females (steps C

steps.title: and locate the female reproductive tract (steps C3–6)
steps.instruction: and locate the female reproductive tract (steps C3–6).

steps.title: Cut the pubic bone and then...
steps.instruction: Cut the pubic bone and then cut between the vagina and anus using large scissors ( Figure 4B ).

steps.title: Lift the vagina with tweezers (...
steps.instruction: Lift the vagina with tweezers ( Figure 4C ) and use small scissors to collect the female reproductive tract, including the vagina, into a 60 mm dish ( Figure 4D ). Figure

steps.title: Collection of the uterus post-ejaculation
steps.instruction: Collection of the uterus post-ejaculation. (A) Cut pubic bone. (B) Cut between the vagina and anus. (C, D) The uterus collected post-ejaculation. (E) Ligation of the upper side of the vaginal cervix. (F) Uterus placed between filter papers and fixed. (G) Cutting of uterus.

steps.title: Use tweezers to ligate the upper...
steps.instruction: Use tweezers to ligate the upper side of the vaginal cervix (uterine side) with silk thread.

steps.title: Place the female reproductive tract between...
steps.instruction: Place the female reproductive tract between filter papers and fix with Bouin's solution overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Wash the female reproductive tract with...
steps.instruction: Wash the female reproductive tract with 1× PBS for 10 min at room temperature and replace with 30% (w/v) sucrose solution overnight at 4 °C.

steps.title: Cut the uterus using small scissors...
steps.instruction: Cut the uterus using small scissors and embed in a cryomold using OCT compound.

steps.title: Freeze the samples in liquid nitrogen...
steps.instruction: Freeze the samples in liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C until use.

steps.title: Prepare 5 μm sections using the...
steps.instruction: Prepare 5 μm sections using the cryostat and attach to glass slides.

steps.title: Dry the sections thoroughly at room...
steps.instruction: Dry the sections thoroughly at room temperature, then wash with tap water for 10 min.

steps.title: Stain the sections with hematoxylin solution...
steps.instruction: Stain the sections with hematoxylin solution for 5 min, wash with tap water, and stain with an eosin solution for 2 min.

steps.title: Wash the sections with tap water...
steps.instruction: Wash the sections with tap water and sequentially immerse in 70%, 80%, 90%, 100%, and 100% ethanol, xylene, and xylene for 30 s each.

steps.title: Add a few drops of New...
steps.instruction: Add a few drops of New M·X to the sample, and cover with a cover glass.

steps.title: Capture brightfield images of the uterus...
steps.instruction: Capture brightfield images of the uterus using a microscope ( Figure 5 ). Figure

steps.title: Histological analysis of the uterus post-ejaculation
steps.instruction: Histological analysis of the uterus post-ejaculation. Histological analysis of the uterus during estrus and post-ejaculation using H&E staining. Dotted box, image enlarged below; scale bars = 100 μm.

description: Validation of protocol Part of this protocol is an optimized version of the method used in previous articles: Kawano et al. [6]. Seminal vesicle protein SVS2 is required for sperm survival in the uterus. Proc Natl Acad Sci USA . (SI Appendix, Figures S9–S13) Matsumoto et al. [7]. Dynamics of post-ejaculated intrauterine environment in mice. MicroPubl Biol . (Figure 1)
